text.title: Supabase MVP Stack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Supabase MVP Stack application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Row-Level Security (RLS) ON for every table from creation. RLS-off tables are an immediate data-leak path.
rule.rationale: Default-off is the most common Supabase mistake; default-on policy prevents the worst class of leaks.

rule.statement: Policies SELECT specific columns / rows. Avoid `TO public USING (true)` — always scope to `auth.uid()` or role.
rule.rationale: Permissive policies create public-data leaks; explicit policies are the safe baseline.

rule.statement: All schema changes via versioned migrations (supabase migration new). Manual SQL in dashboard ⇒ drift + ungovernable.
rule.rationale: Manual schema changes orphan local dev environments + create irreproducible bugs.

rule.statement: Storage buckets have explicit policies + public-private distinction. Default to private + signed URLs.
rule.rationale: Public-by-default storage is the second-most-common Supabase leak; explicit policy prevents.

rule.statement: Realtime subscriptions have payload + per-row policy mindset. Don't broadcast tables with PII to anonymous clients.
rule.rationale: Realtime over-broadcast is a quiet PII-leak vector; explicit consideration prevents.
